title: Carlsberg V Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby Secure Solid Away Win in Historical Northampton Clash.

Cold Ashby Bowls Club braved the pre-match elements to secure a convincing 46-35 shot victory against Carlsberg, played at one of the town's most historic bowling venues.

Despite an unsettling pre-match downpour that threatened to slick the green, the weather cleared just in time for the opening ends, remaining dry and clear throughout the contest.

Cold Ashby found their rhythm early on the historic surface, establishing control across the rinks and maintaining a steady lead for the majority of the match. Carlsberg put up a spirited fight on their home turf, but Cold Ashby’s consistency proved too much, ultimately sealing an 11-shot victory.

The standout performance of the day came from the trio of Gerry, Ollie, and Michael, who claimed the honor of highest winning rink with an excellent display of draw bowling that kept the pressure firmly on the hosts.

title: West Haddon V Cold Ashby
content: On Thursday we travelled the short distance to play at West Haddon. On a lovely summer evening all players enjoyed the match. Cold Ashby were in control for all the match, winning on three out of the four rinks. The final match score was Cold Ashby 75 West Haddon 52. For a change the winning rink was not skipped by Roger Miller. It was Damian Pidgeon with fellow team members Guy and Dean.

title: Local Derby Delight as Cold Ashby Dominate South Kilworth
content:
Cold Ashby made the short six-mile trip to near-neighbours South Kilworth this weekend, returning home with a convincing and hard-fought victory.







From the very first wood, Cold Ashby took firm control of the contest. The team showed relentless consistency across the green, refusing to let their local rivals find a rhythm. That steady pressure paid off handsomely by the final end, with Cold Ashby securing a comfortable 20-shot victory, finishing 79 shots to 59.

The standout performance of the day came from the rink of Roger (again), Guy, Stewart, and Gareth. In superb form, they spearheaded the team's effort, with Roger continuing an impressive personal winning streak to anchor a fantastic afternoon of bowls.

An excellent team performance all round, keeping the early-season momentum firmly in Cold Ashby's favor!

title: Venue: Desborough (Away)
content:
Final Score: Desborough 71 – 47 Cold Ashby

It was a challenging afternoon on the road for Cold Ashby, as the visitors struggled to come to terms with some very difficult playing conditions at Desborough.

The home team took advantage of their local knowledge, establishing control of the match right from the opening ends. Despite Desborough driving home their advantage across most of the green, Cold Ashby showed plenty of fight and managed to secure a well-earned winning rink.

The trio of Roger Miller, Pedro Owen, and Stuart put in an excellent performance to defeat their counterparts and provide a bright spot in a tough away fixture.

title: Cold Ashby v Geddington
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather

title: Rothwell Manor V Cold Ashby
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather

title: Bugbrooke v Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby endured a tough afternoon on the road, facing not only a strong opponent but also punishingly hot and sultry conditions.
The elements played a major factor in the contest, with the stifling heat testing the stamina of both teams from the very first end.
Ultimately, it was the much younger Bugbrooke home side that adapted better to the heavy, humid atmosphere and their own home surface.
Bugbrooke managed to find their rhythm early on and maintained a consistent green speed, gradually pulling away on the scoreboard to run out comfortable winners by a final score of 92 shots to 70.

title: Club Competitions & BBQ
content:
Our scheduled opponents unfortunately had to cancel as they were unable to field a team.

However, the blank Saturday did not go to waste. The afternoon was instead used to advance our internal club competitions, producing several incredibly tense, down-to-the-wire matches:

Rick Singleton vs. Guy Russell: A fantastic battle that saw Guy edge out a victory by just 2 shots.
David Smith vs. Pedro Owen: An even closer affair, with David ultimately clinching the win by a single shot.
With the intense competitive matches wrapped up, members headed round to "Pedro's Palace" to enjoy the sunshine with a hard-earned barbecue and a friendly game of croquet.

Sunday 24th May: Club 4s Competition
The scorching weather continued into Sunday for the highly anticipated Club 4s competition.
The day featured a stark contrast between the runaway leaders and the rest of the field.
The Winners: The powerhouse team of Matt, Rebecca, Lesley, and skip Roger dominated the green, winning the competition by a country mile.
The Runners-Up: Guy, Harry, Dean, and skip Rick fought hard to claim second place, finishing 15 shots adrift of the clinical winners

title: Cold Ashby V Rugby Railway
content:
On a bitterly cold evening, Cold Ashby entertained one of the strongest teams in the area. Despite the drop in temperature, the home side put up a gallant fight from the opening ends, showing fantastic resilience against highly clinical opposition. In the end, the visitors' depth proved too much to match, with Rugby Railway taking the overall victory.

Rink Highlight: The Fighting Trio
The definitive highlight of the night for Cold Ashby came from a brilliant performance by Stuart, Harry, and Graham. Battling both the tough conditions and a formidable away lineup, the trio showcased exceptional accuracy and teamwork to master their green, securing a fantastic rink win for the home club and keeping the final scoreboard respectable

title: Whyte Melville V Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby traveled to Fernie Fields on Sunday—the home of Whyte Melville Bowls Club—for a closely contested match. Despite a forecast threatening rain, the weather held out, allowing for an uninterrupted afternoon of bowls.

After 16 ends of highly competitive play, the home team secured victory, defeating the visitors by 72 shots to 64.

While Whyte Melville took the overall win, the standout rink for the visitors was the trio of Graham, Dean, and Pedro, who claimed a rink win for Cold Ashby.

title: Harpole V Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby traveled to Harpole on Saturday for a closely contested encounter. While the final scoreline suggests a relatively tight margin of 16 shots, the narrative of the match was defined by one extraordinary performance from the visitors that kept the overall tally competitive against a dominant Harpole side.

The Match Dynamics
For much of the afternoon, Harpole controlled the majority of the rinks. The home side utilized their familiarity with the green to build steady leads across the board. On most fronts, Harpole appeared to be heading for a runaway victory.

The "Standout" Rink: Guy, Lesley, and Graham
The highlight of the day for Cold Ashby was the sensational performance of Guy, Lesley, and Graham. In a display of clinical bowling, the trio completely dismantled their Harpole counterparts. Their synergy resulted in a massive 23-shot swing (winning their rink 30–7), keeping the overall match score within 16 shots

title: North Kilworth vs Cold Ashby
content:
In a challenging away fixture, Cold Ashby fell short against a disciplined North Kilworth side. The story of the day was the heavy rink at North Kilworth requiring significant weight and precise touch.

Rink Highlight: The Veteran Masterclass

Despite the overall scoreboard favoring the home side, the rink of Matt, Harry, and Roger provided a masterclass in tactical bowling. Displaying composure that comes with years of experience, they were the only Cold Ashby unit to truly tame the heavy green, finishing with a commanding 28–9 victory

title: Daventry V Cold Ashby
content:
The Comeback: Successfully snapped a two-game losing streak.
Final Score: Cold Ashby 84 | Daventry 75 'Standout' Performance: Chris, Harry, and Damo winning their rink

title: Cold Ashby V L.V.A.
content:
Under a cloudless sky, Cold Ashby entertained L.V.A. in what proved to be a highly competitive afternoon. The home team iltimately lost narrowly by 56 shots to 51


The "standout" performance came from the winning rink of Rick, Harry and guest player Pete Ridley

title: Club Competion Report. The Triples Cup
content:
The season kicked off in spectacular fashion with the first of our internal club competitions, the Triples Cup. The day was filled with intense matches, showcasing the skill and determination of our members.

The Winners: The formidable team of Roger, Dean and, and Harry emerged victorious, demonstrating exceptional teamwork and strategy throughout the competition.

The Runners-Up: Ollie, Matt, and Harry put up a valiant effort, finishing just behind the winners in a closely contested final.

title: Season Opener V Fleckney
content:
It was a nail-bitting encounter that went down to the final woods. In a remarkably close contest, Cold Ashby lost by just one shot. Fleckney 71 Cold Ashby 70.


The home teams 'top-performing' rink featured Chris,Stuart and Damo

title: Meet and Greet
content:
Club members held their 'pre-season' meet and greet, welcoming new members and providing an opportunity for everyone to get to know each other before the competitive season began.


The event was a great success, fostering camaraderie and team spirit among the members.


The event included the first roll up on the green, which is in superb condition
